title: Weather Today [24 March, 2026]: Check IMD Forecast, Snow, Heat Across States & Rain Predictions for Delhi NCR, Mumbai, Chennai, Karnataka, Rajasthan, Jammu & Ahmedabad - The Sunday Guardian
full_text:
Weather Today (24 March 2026): Check IMD forecast for Delhi NCR, Mumbai, Chennai, Karnataka, Rajasthan, J&K, Ahmedabad, Punjab, Hyderabad & UP. Get city-wise temperature, rain, snow predictions & sunrise-sunset timings.
Weather Today (24 March 2026): India’s weather on March 24, 2026, shows a mix of clear skies, rising temperatures, and localized rainfall in the northeast. Northern plains and southern peninsular states will largely remain dry while the Himalayas may see light snowfall and temperatures across major cities range from a minimum of 16°C to a maximum of 39°C.
Northern hilly regions like Uttarakhand and Ladakh may see light snowfall or rainfall. No major disturbances are expected, but travelers should remain cautious on mountain roads. The minimum temperature in these regions will hover around 2°C, with maximum temperatures reaching 7°C.
Minimum: 22°C | Maximum: 31°C
Delhi is expected to experience mostly clear skies with mild morning winds. Afternoon temperatures will rise slightly and contributing to warm conditions by late afternoon.
Minimum: 27°C | Maximum: 34°C
Chennai will remain dry with partly cloudy skies and coastal breezes are expected, keeping humidity levels manageable despite rising daytime temperatures.
Minimum: 28°C | Maximum: 31°C
Mumbai sees stable weather with warm temperatures throughout the day and wind speeds are around 7.59 km/h from the northwest with gusts reaching 11.1 km/h.
Minimum: 24°C | Maximum: 35°C
Hyderabad is set for hot afternoons with partly cloudy skies. The wind is expected at 4.55 km/h from the southeast with occasional gusts of 7.57 km/h.
Minimum: 22°C | Maximum: 33°C
Most of Karnataka will witness dry weather with intermittent cloud cover and coastal areas may feel slightly humid, but no rainfall is forecast.
Minimum: 21°C | Maximum: 32°C
Uttar Pradesh will experience clear skies with pleasant mornings and warm afternoons. Wind speeds around 7.61 km/h will keep conditions comfortable.
Minimum: 21°C | Maximum: 31°C
Rajasthan will see patchy cloud cover, particularly in western regions. Temperatures remain moderate, though afternoons will feel warmer under clear skies.
Minimum: -4°C | Maximum: 5°C
Srinagar may experience light snow, especially in elevated areas. Winds from the west at 2.78 km/h will make mornings feel colder.
Minimum: 18°C | Maximum: 31°C
Punjab will have mostly clear skies with comfortable morning temperatures and warm afternoons. Winds from the northwest at 7.43 km/h are expected.
Minimum: 25°C | Maximum: 37°C
Ahmedabad is likely to experience hot and dry conditions. Daytime temperatures may reach 37°C, with clear skies prevailing across the city.
Rising daytime temperatures will affect energy consumption and outdoor activities, particularly in northern and western India. Farmers and commuters should take precautions during peak afternoon hours.
Delhi’s AQI is moderate at 153, with particulate matter (PM2.5) levels at 92 µg/m³ and PM10 at 180 µg/m³. Residents are advised to limit outdoor activity for sensitive groups.
1. Are there any travel or safety warnings?
Yes, northern hilly regions may experience light snow and drivers should exercise caution.
2. Will temperatures rise across the country?
Yes, daytime temperatures are expected to increase, particularly in north and west India.
3. Will there be any rain today?
Rain is mainly expected in northeastern states and most of the country remains dry.
4. Where will it snow today?
Snowfall may occur in higher-altitude areas of Jammu and Kashmir and Uttarakhand.
5. How will air quality affect outdoor activities?
Moderate AQI in Delhi and other cities suggests sensitive groups limit outdoor exposure.
